title: Dense Kenyan Biscuit Clay Pot
description: A dense, moist, and flavorful biscuit-like dish from Kenya, perfect for a weekend brunch. With a subtle mustard flavor and no onions or garlic in sight!

ingredients.name: bicarbonate of soda
ingredients.notes: make sure it's fresh for best results

instructions: Preheat a tandoor clay pot to 400°F (200°C).
In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the flour, bicarbonate of soda, salt, and sugar.
Add the softened butter and use a pastry blender or your fingers to work it into the dry ingredients until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the egg, yogurt, mustard seeds, and water.
Add the yeast to the wet ingredients and stir to combine.
Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and mix until a shaggy dough forms.
Knead the dough on a floured surface for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in the preheated clay pot and cover with a lid.
Bake for 35-40 minutes or until the biscuits are golden brown and a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Remove from the oven and let cool for 10 minutes before slicing and serving.
Garnish with black sesame seeds if desired.
